summary: Middle East conflict escalation — including attacks on energy infrastructure and Strait of Hormuz disruption fears — has jolted inflation expectations and compressed rate-cut bets across markets. A hotter-than-expected PPI print this week (+0.7% MoM) has flipped market rate expectations, shifting from a potential cut to a potential hike in 2026.
tweet: A surprise PPI surge and Strait of Hormuz fears have flipped rate-cut bets to potential hike bets — fear is spreading from bonds into stocks and crushing gold's safe-haven appeal.
